This file is the promotion payload, not evidence that `v0.2.0` is public.
Publication is established only by the immutable GitHub Release metadata and
its exact verified assets.

Veqtor v0.2.0 is a community-supported technical Alpha focused on safer
contract-review workflows and easier activation in Claude Desktop for macOS.

Highlights:

- a versioned macOS Claude Desktop Extension (`.mcpb`) with explicit
  tracked-change-author configuration, the same six Veqtor tools, a synthetic
  four-round demo and a ready-to-run first prompt;
- closed MCP input and output schemas, including a proof-bound preflight/apply
  flow that detects changes to the source, edit request, author or predicted
  output between the two calls;
- complete revision-accounting fields and explicit diagnostics for unsupported
  tracked-change structures;
- deterministic round ordering with an optional complete filename manifest,
  while making clear that filename order does not prove chronology or lineage;
- fail-closed decision-record workspace discovery and a privacy-minimized
  compact export that keeps raw document text and the private journal local;
- reproducible wheel, source archive and MCPB builds. `SHA256SUMS.txt` binds all
  three release payloads; the checksum is an integrity check, not a digital
  signature.

The first extension activation may download a compatible Python runtime and the
dependencies pinned in `uv.lock` through Claude Desktop's UV runtime. The
extension is a macOS activation path; the version-pinned `uvx` and
`uv tool install` paths remain available for supported command-line
installations.

To install in Claude Desktop, download and open
`veqtor-mcp-0.2.0-macos.mcpb`, approve the extension, and enter the name that
Word should show as the author of new tracked changes. Then run the bundled
`try_veqtor_demo` prompt:

> Using the Veqtor tools, what happened to the limitation of liability across
> the four Word rounds in the folder named demo? Start with list_rounds. Keep
> tool-confirmed document facts separate from your interpretation, and do not
> treat filename order as verified chronology.

For an optional advanced integrity check, compare the output of
`shasum -a 256 veqtor-mcp-0.2.0-macos.mcpb` with its entry in
`SHA256SUMS.txt`. The MCPB is not digitally signed. Because this is the first
public MCPB, there is no older extension to roll back to; this release tests
clean install, uninstall, post-uninstall tool absence and same-artifact
reinstall. Real upgrade and rollback become release gates for the next MCPB.

Veqtor remains a local technical Alpha. It is not legal advice, a hosted
service, a full Word editor, a chronology/lineage proof system or a
tamper-evident audit log. Review the
[known limitations](https://github.com/JohnDeer-ai/veqtor-mcp/blob/v0.2.0/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md)
before using it on a real matter.
